title: Small Cap Growth Investment Thesis - Client 021
content: Small-capitalization stocks offer superior growth potential for investors willing to accept higher volatility and conduct thorough due diligence. Market Opportunity: Small cap universe ($300M-$2B market capitalization) contains innovative companies with significant expansion runways. Statistical advantages include lower analyst coverage creating pricing inefficiencies and acquisition targets commanding premium valuations. Investment Criteria: Revenue growth exceeding 20% annually, positive cash flow or clear path to profitability, strong management teams with aligned incentives, addressable markets exceeding $5 billion, competitive moats through technology or network effects. Sector Focus: Software-as-a-Service (35%) with scalable business models and recurring revenue. Cybersecurity (20%) addressing growing enterprise needs. Financial Technology (20%) disrupting traditional banking. Healthcare Technology (15%) improving care delivery efficiency. Industrial Automation (10%) driving manufacturing productivity. Portfolio Construction: Diversify across minimum 25-30 holdings to reduce single-stock risk. Client-021's $1.5M allocation enables meaningful positions while maintaining diversification. Use blend of direct stock ownership (70%) and small-cap growth ETFs (30%) for broader exposure. Risk Management: Quarterly fundamental reviews, strict sell discipline on companies missing growth targets, geographic diversification, avoid illiquid stocks. Expected returns: 18-28% annually with higher volatility during market corrections. 5-7 year minimum holding period recommended.
